entity: The Metropolitan Water District of Southern California
summary: Authorize GM to enter LRP Agreement with Eastern Municipal Water District for French Valley Recycled Water Distribution Project: 25-year term, 140 AFY contract yield, fixed incentive up to $305/AF over 25 years.
quote: Fixed incentive of up to $305/AF over 25 years.

entity: The Metropolitan Water District of Southern California
summary: Extend the Metropolitan/Quechan Tribe Seasonal Fallowing Pilot to incentivize fallowing up to $864,000 in 2024, paying $530.61 per irrigable acre; estimated savings ~2 AF per acre, up to 1,600 acres.
quote: the Pilot will continue to incentivize farmers to fallow land ... at $530.61 per irrigable acre in 2024

entity: The Metropolitan Water District of Southern California
summary: Agreements to conserve 351,000 AF of Colorado River system water through fallowing 2023–2026; Reclamation to pay Metropolitan $400/AF for conserved water left in Lake Mead; payments shared with Palo Verde community.
quote: Reclamation would make payments to Metropolitan of $400 per AF of conserved water left in Lake Mead.

entity: The Metropolitan Water District of Southern California
summary: Authorize GM to secure one-year water transfers and exchanges with various districts for up to $50 million; cites prior 2022 purchases of 16,223 AF at $800/AF and 3,825 AF at $447/AF.
quote: Authorize the General Manager to secure one-year water transfers and water exchanges with various water districts for up to $50 million

entity: The Metropolitan Water District of Southern California
summary: Authorize General Manager to enter Local Resources Program Agreement with Las Virgenes MWD and JPA for PURE Water Project for up to 5,000 AFY; fixed incentive up to $340/AF over 25 years.
quote: Fixed incentive of up to $340/AF over 25 years.

entity: The Metropolitan Water District of Southern California
summary: Local Resources Program offers incentives of up to $250/AF to expand water recycling and groundwater recovery; 86 projects expected to produce about 363,000 AFY; MWD has provided over $244 million historically.
quote: Providing incentives of up to $250 per acre-foot to expand water recycling and groundwater recovery programs.

entity: The Metropolitan Water District of Southern California
summary: Authorize GM to enter LRP Agreement with LADWP for Los Angeles Groundwater Replenishment Project for up to 19,500 AFY; Metropolitan's maximum obligation up to $138,937,500 at $475/AF over 15 years.
quote: Metropolitan’s maximum financial obligation would be up to $138,937,500 for a Project yield of 19,500 AFY at $475/AF over 15 years.

entity: The Metropolitan Water District of Southern California
summary: Metropolitan seeks forbearance to allow Reclamation-CVWD agreements funding reduced groundwater replenishment up to 35,000 AF/year and agricultural conservation 2024–2026 at $400/AF; includes related IID/SDCWA provisions up to 100,000 AF/year.
quote: forbearance is needed for an agreement between Reclamation and CVWD to fund a reduction in groundwater replenishment by up to 35,000 acre-feet per year in 2024 and 2025 at $400 per acre-foot.

entity: The Metropolitan Water District of Southern California
summary: Proposed one-time option payments to secure first right of refusal for water transfers from Western Canal WD and Richvale ID for 2025–2027; $250,000 each; transfer prices $965/AF (<=20% SWP) and $600/AF (>20%).
quote: The price for water made available and called is $965 per acre-foot at SWP allocations of 20 percent or less and $600 per acre-foot at SWP allocations greater than 20 percent.

entity: Los Angeles Department of Water and Power
summary: LADWP to enter Agreement No. AO-5302 with MWD for Local Resources Program participation, providing up to $139,350,750; MWD funds $475/AF for first 15 years to recharge San Fernando Basin.
quote: The Agreement will provide LADWP a financial incentive of up to $139,350,750.

entity: The Metropolitan Water District of Southern California
summary: Authorize GM to enter into Reverse-Cyclic Program agreements to defer deliveries of up to 50,000 AF in 2024 and up to 50,000 AF in 2025, generating new revenues to help achieve $60 million in one-time revenues.
quote: defer deliveries of up to 50,000 acre-feet in calendar year 2024 and up to 50,000 acre-feet in calendar year 2025.

entity: The Metropolitan Water District of Southern California
summary: Authorize GM to enter into agreements with U.S. Bureau of Reclamation for phase two of the Lower Colorado Conservation Program to implement AVEK HDWB and Turf Replacement, yielding long-term conserved water for Lake Mead.
quote: create a total of 265,296 acre-feet of conserved water to benefit Lake Mead as system water

entity: The Metropolitan Water District of Southern California
summary: Authorize GM to enter into funding agreement with U.S. Bureau of Reclamation under the Lower Colorado Conservation and Efficiency Program to implement phase two DAC Leak Detection and Repair, receiving federal funding and creating conserved water.
quote: create a total of 4,000 acre-feet of conserved water to benefit Lake Mead

entity: The Metropolitan Water District of Southern California
summary: Presentation recommending extension of PVID voluntary five-month fallowing with federal funding to add conserved water to Lake Mead; federal funding at $400/AF.
quote: Federal funding: $400/AF

summary: Authorize agreement with Palo Verde Irrigation District; extend PVID fallowing program and receive USBR LC Conservation funding for system conservation water.
quote: All system conservation water under this extension will receive USBR’s payment of $400 per AF

summary: Authorize expansion of Bard Seasonal Fallowing Program to 6,000 acres and amend SCIA; Reclamation to pay $400/AF for conserved CR water left in Lake Mead.
quote: Reclamation will provide a payment of $400 per AF of conserved water left in Lake Mead.

entity: The Metropolitan Water District of Southern California
summary: Authorize General Manager to forbear water conserved by two CVWD projects so conserved water can be added to Lake Mead under Reclamation’s LC Conservation Program.
quote: reduction in groundwater replenishment by up to 35,000 acre-feet per year at $400 per acre-foot

entity: The Metropolitan Water District of Southern California
summary: Amendment covering exchange of water 2027–2035; Coachella to pay $400 per AF for exchange with options to pre-deliver up to 200,000 AF and deliver 315,000 AF by 2035.

entity: Southern Nevada Water Authority
summary: For FY2025-26 SNWA increased the wholesale delivery charge for treated water from $396 to $407 per acre-foot.
quote: For fiscal year 2025-26, the wholesale delivery charge for treated water sold will rise by $22 per acre-foot. This increases the charge from $396 to $407 per acre-foot.

entity: Southern Nevada Water Authority
summary: Wholesale Delivery Charge was $396/AF for FY ended June 30, 2025 and $382/AF for FY2024; non-potable charged $300/AF; agreement allows sublease of 3,000 AF per year.
quote: During the fiscal years ended June 30, 2025 and 2024, the Wholesale Delivery Charge was $396 and $382 per acre-foot (AF) of potable water, respectively.

entity: City of Bullhead City
summary: Adopted Comprehensive Fee Schedule listing 'All other uses' water charge of $405.00 per acre-foot of estimated annual use.
quote: All other uses* *Per acre foot of estimated annual use $405.00

entity: Southern Nevada Water Authority
summary: For FY2026-27 SNWA increased the wholesale delivery charge for treated water from $407 to $419 per acre-foot.
quote: For fiscal year 2026-27, the wholesale delivery charge for treated water sold will rise by $12 per acre-foot. This increases the charge from $407 to $419 per acre-foot.

entity: The Metropolitan Water District of Southern California
summary: Authorized General Manager to enter SCIA with Reclamation to add up to 200,000 AF to Lake Mead in 2026; Reclamation to compensate Metropolitan for conserved water.
quote: add up to 200,000 acre-feet (AF) of Colorado River system water to Lake Mead in 2026. Metropolitan would be compensated at $325 per AF

entity: Palo Verde Irrigation District
summary: PVID board to discuss Reclamation's offer to extend SCIA Bucket 1 at $325/acre-foot through the 2027-2028 water year.

entity: Southern Nevada Water Authority

entity: Southern Nevada Water Authority
summary: Board to approve system conservation implementation agreement creating up to 50,000 acre-feet of System Conservation Water in exchange for $16,250,000 in federal funding.
